label: Squarish-shell 2-STOREY 3BR / 2 baths, REAR STAIR, hall-less ground floor, distributed baths (one per floor) — completes the 2s 3BR shell set. Ground floor: great room across the front (entry), stair + kitchen + T&B tiling the rear zone. Upstairs: master + second bedroom street-facing at the front, third bedroom at the rear, landing hall as a compact core serving all five doors. ORIENTATION-ADAPTIVE stair: the solver lays the flight sideways along the rear band on 7 m-wide floors and flips it to a vertical rear-corner run at 6.5 m and below. Canonical 10.5x10.5 lot (6.5x6.5 buildable per floor, 84 m² gross — 3BR/2-bath band 80-120).

adjacencies.note: GF: kitchen opens directly off the great room — dining counter on the shared edge (2 x 0.6 m seats + 0.9 m pass = 2.1 m min); band + stools live on the great-room side to keep the galley aisle clear. Render-only (counter_divider) — briefs may set dining_counter: false to suppress it without touching this topology.
GF: T&B doors into the great room — hall-less GF, the great room IS the circulation; door-placement scoring keeps it off the entry sightline
GF: open stair boards straight off the great room; the boarding constraint holds this edge to the flight's bottom end
GF: T&B shares a plumbing wall with the kitchen — single GF wet bay at the rear; kitchen keeps its rear service door
VERTICAL circulation edge: flight and stairwell opening occupy the identical rectangle on their floors (rect-equality in the solver, graph connectivity for validation)
2F: the landing hall must meet the stairwell at the TOP end of the flight — step off into circulation, never a bedroom wall
2F: master door off the landing hall
2F: second bedroom door off the landing hall
2F: third bedroom door off the landing hall-core
2F: second T&B doors off the landing hall

soft_proximities.note: 2F: pull the upper T&B toward the rear stair corner — lands it near the GF wet bay (kitchen + bath1 share the rear band), shortening the vertical plumbing run. Soft only: cross-floor wet-stack alignment isn't a hard v2 constraint yet.

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: stair_run_geometry
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: ALWAYS-ON (the 99999 trigger): a straight stair's run is riser math the solver cannot derive — 3.0 m floor-to-floor at 15 x 200 mm risers needs 14 x 250 mm treads = 3.5 m; min_greatest_dim_m pins the run, max_least_dim_m 1.2 keeps the flight a flight; ORIENTATION stays the solver's choice, and on this squarish shell it genuinely varies by lot size (sideways at 7 m wide, vertical at 6.5 m and below — a 6.5 m rear band can't fit stair 3.5 + counter-width kitchen 2.1 + bath side by side). common_bath caps stop post-snap bath stretching. hallway max_area_sqm 6.0: the five-door hall-core earns more than the 2BR siblings' 4.5 but stays bounded.

notes: 2-STOREY squarish catalog topology #2 — the squarish take on the 2s
3BR (authored 2026-07-19; completes 2s coverage of {2BR,3BR} x
{narrow, squarish, wide}). Same room graph as the wide 3BR sibling;
the shell does the differentiating.
Program: 3BR / 2 baths DISTRIBUTED one per floor (baths_ds) — GF
common T&B off the great room, upper common bath off the hall-core.
All three bedrooms upstairs; master + br2 street-facing at the front,
br3 at the rear.
ORIENTATION-ADAPTIVE STAIR (the finding this topology surfaced): the
solver's run-axis decision flips with lot size — sideways along the
rear band on 7x7 floors (band fits stair 3.5 + kitchen + bath), a
vertical rear-corner column at 6.5x6.5 and below (band too narrow).
Both orientations verified COMPLIANT; nothing in this file forces
either.
Canonical layout (10.5 x 10.5 lot, 2 m setbacks, 6.5 x 6.5 buildable
per floor — vertical-stair form):
GF:  Front: [great room (entry, full width, counter at rear edge)]
Rear:  [stair | kitchen  -> rear service door | T&B         ]
2F:  Front: [br2   | master                                      ]
Mid:   [stairwell | hall-core | br3                         ]
Rear:  [stairwell | T&B       | br3                         ]
Verified sizes (all COMPLIANT, 0 warnings, deterministic):
- 10.5x10.5 canonical: 84 m² gross, 100% coverage both floors;
master 12.0 / br2 7.5 / br3 10.5 / bath2 2.4 / hall 6.0.
- 11x11 (98): sideways stair; a ~5 m² strip behind the flight goes
unclaimed on 2F (its only neighbors are excluded claimant types) —
suggestion-level, honest behind-stair space.
- 12x11 (112) and 12x12 (128 — above band): solve cleanly.
- 10x10 (72 — below band): solves; treat the 80 m² band, not solver
feasibility, as the practical floor when matching briefs.
This topology's authoring exposed and fixed a THREE-layer leak of the
locked 'master is always larger' rule (see project memory): (1) the
solver constrained master against only the FIRST standard bedroom —
now against ALL of them; (2) snap_gaps could grow a standard past the
master — now capped at master's current area minus half the margin;
(3) the dead-strip claimer could hand a standard an alcove that
outgrew the master — now guarded. Post-snap margins land at >= 0.5 m²
(solver enforces 1.0 on the pre-snap rects).
Structure/override choices: identical to the wide 3BR sibling — only
the stair is anchored (single-member left_anchored), two gentle stacks
([master, hall2], [br2, hall2]), zone_balance_rooms counts the whole
upper floor minus stairwell as private, no zone_split, kitchen
mechanical_vent, soft proximity bath2->stairwell for the wet stack.
Naming: `baths_ds` = distributed baths, `gr` = open great-room style,
no `_hall` token (GROUND floor is hall-less; the 2F landing hall is
mandatory in every 2s topology and is not tokened).
